title: Baton Journey Attestations
description: Durable record of human walkthroughs of critical customer journeys (Rule 10). The model CANNOT author these — walked_by is human-set at the cutover gate. The no-mock boundary is what makes a walkthrough proof rather than theatre. This is the record *shape* (protocol); attestation data and any telemetry derived from it are not.

version.description: Artefact schema version for forward compatibility.

ratification.description: Human ratification of the attestations artefact as a whole (parallels journeys-v1.ratification), distinct from any single attestation's per-walkthrough boundary. Optional: projects that gate on individual attestation status rather than a file-level sign-off may omit this.

boundary.description: Describes the deployment context these attestations were walked against — distinct from each attestation's own per-walkthrough boundary object (which asserts real_infra/mocks_off for that one walkthrough). Optional.

mock_banned.description: True when no mocks or stubs are permitted anywhere in this boundary.

entitlement_boundary.description: The entitlement boundary path or identifier this context enforces.

journey_id.description: References a journey id in journeys-v1.

walked_by.description: Who performed the walkthrough. Mandatory for walked-pass / walked-fail (human-set).

boundary.description: The no-mock boundary assertions — what makes a walkthrough proof rather than theatre. Grouped so it can grow (e.g. which boundaries were exercised) without a breaking change.

real_infra.description: Asserts the walkthrough ran against real infrastructure.

mocks_off.description: Asserts no mock or stub was active during the walkthrough.

notes.description: Free-form observations from the human walker.
